entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Global shift in weather patterns impacting food security and agricultural productivity

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to a sufficient quantity of affordable, nutritious food

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: Country in South Asia where the study is focused

entities.name: GREEN REVOLUTION
entities.description: A period of significant increase in crop productivity through intensification

entities.name: RICE-WHEAT SYSTEM
entities.description: An intensive cereal cropping system common in the Indo-Gangetic Plain

entities.name: INDO-GANGETIC PLAIN
entities.description: A fertile region in South Asia serving as a major agricultural hub

entities.name: SOUTH ASIA
entities.description: Geographic region containing India and surrounding countries

entities.name: CONSERVATION AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Management practices including no-tillage, residue retention, and diversified rotations to improve soil resilience

entities.name: NO-TILLAGE
entities.description: Agricultural practice of growing crops without disturbing the soil through tillage

entities.name: RESIDUE RETENTION
entities.description: The practice of leaving crop residues on the soil surface to protect the soil

entities.name: DIVERSIFIED ROTATIONS
entities.description: The practice of planting a variety of crops in sequence to improve soil health

entities.name: CROP YIELD
entities.description: The amount of agricultural production harvested per unit area

entities.name: NORTHWEST INDIA
entities.description: Specific region of India where the conservation agriculture study was conducted

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A primary cereal crop used in the intensive cropping systems of India

entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A primary cereal crop used in the intensive cropping systems of India

entities.name: MUNG BEAN
entities.description: A legume crop used in diversified rotations to enhance system productivity

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: A cereal crop used in diversified rotations to enhance system productivity

entities.name: PUDDLED TRANSPLANTED RICE
entities.description: A traditional rice cultivation method involving soil puddling and transplanting

entities.name: ZERO TILL
entities.description: A tillage method that minimizes soil disturbance during planting

entities.name: DIRECT SEEDED RICE
entities.description: A rice planting method where seeds are sown directly into the field

entities.name: SUBSURFACE DRIP IRRIGATION
entities.description: An irrigation system that delivers water directly to the plant root zone

entities.name: RICE EQUIVALENT YIELD
entities.description: A metric used to compare the productivity of different crops relative to rice

entities.name: WRICKE'S ECOVALENCE INDEX
entities.description: A statistical index used to gauge long-term yield stability

entities.name: SUSTAINABILITY YIELD INDEX
entities.description: An index used to evaluate the long-term sustainability of crop yields

entities.name: YIELD STABILITY
entities.description: The consistency of crop yields over time across different seasons

entities.name: SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The ability to maintain agricultural productivity and soil health over the long term

entities.name: SOIL PHYSICAL PROPERTIES
entities.description: The physical characteristics of soil, such as structure and density

entities.name: BULK DENSITY
entities.description: The mass of dry soil per unit volume, indicating soil compaction

entities.name: WATER INFILTRATION RATE
entities.description: The speed at which water enters the soil surface

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC CARBON
entities.description: The amount of carbon stored in soil organic matter

entities.name: SOIL HEALTH
entities.description: The capacity of soil to function as a vital living ecosystem that sustains plants

entities.name: RESILIENCE
entities.description: The ability of the agricultural system to recover from climate-induced stress
